evals.prompt: I need a safe argocd workflow for production. Show me the exact commands to inspect an app, diff it, sync only the out-of-sync resources, and wait for health with a timeout.
evals.expected_output: A command-first answer that uses `argocd app get`, `argocd app diff`, `argocd app sync --apply-out-of-sync-only`, and `argocd app wait --health --sync --operation --timeout ...`, with inspect-before-mutate guidance.

assertions.name: uses-inspect-diff-sync-wait-sequence
assertions.description: The answer follows the inspect -> diff -> sync -> wait sequence rather than jumping straight to mutation.

assertions.name: uses-apply-out-of-sync-only
assertions.description: The answer includes `argocd app sync --apply-out-of-sync-only` for a narrow reconcile.

assertions.name: waits-for-terminal-state
assertions.description: The answer uses `argocd app wait` with health and timeout flags instead of treating sync submission as success.

evals.prompt: Write me a short runbook for Argo CD admin tasks: add a private Git repo with SSH, register a cluster from kubeconfig, and create an AppProject with explicit source and destination guardrails.
evals.expected_output: A concise runbook using `argocd repo add`, `argocd cluster add`, and `argocd proj create` with concrete flags, plus brief scoping and verification steps.

assertions.name: uses-repo-add-with-ssh-key
assertions.description: The answer uses `argocd repo add` with `--ssh-private-key-path` or an equivalent SSH-auth pattern.

assertions.name: uses-cluster-add-from-kube-context
assertions.description: The answer treats `argocd cluster add` as a kubeconfig-context operation, not an app operation.

assertions.name: uses-proj-create-guardrails
assertions.description: The answer uses `argocd proj create` with explicit source repo and destination restrictions.

evals.prompt: My `argocd app diff` step in CI returns exit code 1 and the server is behind an ingress. Explain what that exit code means, when to use `--grpc-web`, and give me a CI-safe command sequence with token auth.
evals.expected_output: An explanation that exit code 1 means a diff exists, not a generic failure, plus a CI-safe command sequence using `ARGOCD_AUTH_TOKEN`, optional `--grpc-web`, and explicit wait/timeout behavior.

assertions.name: explains-diff-exit-code-1
assertions.description: The answer states that `argocd app diff` returns exit code `1` when differences are present.

assertions.name: uses-grpc-web-for-ingress-case
assertions.description: The answer recommends `--grpc-web` for proxy or ingress setups that break native gRPC.

assertions.name: uses-token-auth-in-ci
assertions.description: The answer uses `ARGOCD_AUTH_TOKEN` or `--auth-token` rather than embedding credentials in the script.
